dependencies: Reliance on third-party suppliers for pipe, chemicals, power, fuel, equipment, water and other raw materials
Supply chain disruptions may cause inability to purchase needed goods or services at reasonable prices
Dependence on third-party vendors for technology systems administration and maintenance
risks: Supply chain disruptions related to goods such as pipe, chemicals, power, fuel, equipment, water and other raw materials
Reduced quantities of goods available in marketplace
Delays in manufacturing or shipping goods
Labor shortages at suppliers or vendors
Natural or other disasters and operational impacts to suppliers or vendors
Increased costs of supplies, products and materials critical to business operations

regulatory_risks: Extensive regulation by Public Utility Commissions (PUCs) affecting rates, operations, and financial condition
Potential fines, penalties and sanctions for inability to meet regulatory requirements
Extensive and increasingly stringent environmental, water quality and health and safety laws and regulations
Safe Drinking Water Act compliance and new drinking water standards
Lead and Copper Rule Improvements (LCRI) requiring replacement of all lead and certain galvanized service lines by October 30, 2037
National Primary Drinking Water Regulation (NPDWR) for six PFAS with compliance required by April 2029
PFAS designated as hazardous substances under CERCLA
Clean Water Act regulations on discharges from water and wastewater treatment facilities
NPDES permit requirements and potential violations leading to fines and penalties
Changes in laws and regulations affecting operations, including environmental, health, safety, data privacy, security and water quality standards
State and local regulatory changes and new interpretations of existing regulations
Potential disallowance of cost recovery in rates
Regulatory lag between capital investment/expense increases and rate recovery
PUC approval requirements for acquisitions, debt/equity financing, and affiliated transactions
Condemnation and eminent domain proceedings by municipalities and government entities
Compliance with Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax (CAMT) provisions effective 2024
Potential changes in tax laws and regulations affecting deductibility and tax rates
